briefs.title: BC Sick Note Rules Tightened: Employers Cannot Request Notes for Short Absences
briefs.summary: BC has updated its Employment Standards rules to limit when employers can ask for a sick note (a doctor's certificate proving illness). Employers can no longer request a sick note if an employee's health-related leave is 5 consecutive days or fewer AND the employee has taken no more than one other such short leave in the same calendar year. The rules also expand which health professionals can sign valid sick notes beyond just doctors and nurses.
briefs.action_required: Review and update your sick note policy to stop requesting notes for absences of 5 days or fewer (up to twice per year).

key_dates.label: BC Employment Standards sick note restriction rules take effect
